name: Giant Almond Florentines
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease six 8-inch foil pie pans. Foil pans must be used for easy removal of baked florentines in one piece.
2. In a medium-large heavy saucepan, combine butter, sugar, honey, and cream. Heat to boiling over medium heat, stirring frequently. Cook for 1 1/2 minutes, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Stir in almonds.
3. Divide mixture evenly among greased pie pans. Using the back of a wooden spoon, pat evenly into the bottom of the pans. Bake pans in batches of three on a single oven rack, 10 to 14 minutes, or until a rich golden brown. Rotate pans during baking if necessary to avoid overbrowning. Let cool completely in pans.
4. Refrigerate florentines for 10 to 15 minutes for ease in unmolding.
5. With fingers, carefully press into the pan bottoms from underneath and pop the florentines out in one piece. Refrigerate florentines, stacked between layers of wax paper until thoroughly chilled, about 3 hours.
6. Spread melted chocolate coating over the bottoms of chilled florentines. Let stand until chocolate is set. Wrap each florentine separately in plastic wrap. Store in refrigerator.
Note: Use melted chocolate-coating wafers, which are available at cake decorating and candy supply stores, to spread on the bottoms of florentines for best results. This type of chocolate will stay shiny and give your florentines a professional look. These make fantastic holiday gifts.
